title: Xtra Spicy Thai Papaya Som Tum
description: A winey, silky, monsoon Thai dish featuring bay leaves, this som tum is a classic Isaan recipe with a spicy kick, made gluten-free-friendly with careful ingredient selection.
instructions: Soak dried shrimp in water for 30 minutes, then drain and chop finely.
Combine shredded green papaya, diced tomato, sliced chilies, chopped peanuts, fish sauce, lime juice, palm sugar, crushed bay leaves, and salt in a large mortar.
Pound the mixture with a pestle for about 5 minutes, until the flavors are well combined and the papaya starts to break down.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed, adding more chilies for extra spice or more fish sauce for added depth.
Transfer the som tum to a serving platter or individual plates.
Garnish with additional peanuts, chilies, and a sprinkle of palm sugar, if desired.
Serve immediately, as the flavors will meld together and the texture will become silky over time.
Offer sticky rice or crispy rice cakes on the side, if desired, to soak up the flavorful sauce.
